accessToken.simple: A temporary password that proves you're logged in
accessToken.detail: Expires after ~1 hour. Get it from authSignIn.

idToken.simple: A token containing your user identity information
idToken.detail: Contains claims about who you are. Not used for API auth.

refreshToken.simple: A long-lived token to get new access tokens
refreshToken.detail: Lasts 30 days. Use it to stay logged in without re-entering credentials.

mutation.simple: A request that changes data (like logging in or creating something)
mutation.detail: Unlike queries which just read data, mutations modify it.

query.simple: A request that reads data without changing anything
query.detail: Use queries to fetch campaigns, analytics, etc.

JWT.simple: A secure string containing your identity
JWT.detail: JSON Web Token. Keep it secret—don't share it publicly.

GraphQL.detail: Lets you request exactly the data you need, nothing more.

endpoint.simple: The URL where you send API requests
endpoint.detail: MediaJel's API endpoint is where all GraphQL requests go.

Authorization.simple: The header where you put your access token

Bearer.detail: Always write 'Bearer ' (with a space) before your token.

cURL.simple: A command-line tool for making web requests
cURL.detail: Pre-installed on Mac/Linux. Used in code examples.

organizationId.simple: Your company's unique identifier in MediaJel
organizationId.detail: Used in the 'Key' header. Ask your account manager if unknown.

Cognito.simple: AWS service that handles user login
Cognito.detail: Amazon Cognito manages authentication behind the scenes.

header.simple: Extra information sent with your request
header.detail: Like metadata. Contains auth info, content type, etc.

API.simple: A way for programs to communicate
API.detail: Application Programming Interface. How your code talks to MediaJel.

pagination.simple: Getting results in chunks instead of all at once
pagination.detail: Use 'first', 'skip', or cursor-based params to paginate.

schema.simple: A blueprint of all available operations
schema.detail: Describes what data you can query or mutate.

variable.simple: A placeholder for values in your query
variable.detail: Makes queries reusable. Passed separately from the query.

CTR.detail: Percentage of impressions that resulted in clicks.

ROAS.detail: Revenue generated per dollar spent on advertising.

impressions.simple: Number of times ads were shown
impressions.detail: Each display of your ad counts as one impression.

pace.detail: How fast impressions are being served relative to schedule.

attributionWindow.detail: Days after ad exposure to attribute conversions (default 7-30 days).
